document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD(HAD1417MCW / HAD1717MCW) Digital flat panel x-ray detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: authorized competent authorities and notified bodies
standards: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation report for ADD HAD1417MCW and HAD1717MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors, Rev.2 dated 2022.12.08.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd. and used to support clinical safety, performance, benefit-risk, equivalence, PMS and CE-related essential requirements assessment.
keyPoints: The device is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, not for mammography.
The report states classification as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC.
Clinical evaluation is based on literature review, equivalent predicate devices, PMS data, risk management, and MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4.
The report concludes the risks associated with device use are acceptable when weighed against benefits to the patient.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is regarded as an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device.

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: ADD(HAD1417MCW / HAD1717MCW)Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Post market surveillance
concepts.evidence: The PMS report includes: Customer Complaints Analysis (Latest 5 years), Adverse Events, Experience with Similar Devices Made by the Same or Different Manufacturer, Literature Reviews, PMCF Study

concepts.name: Benefit-risk profile
concepts.evidence: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

concepts.name: Digital flat panel X-ray detector
concepts.evidence: The ADD are wired or wireless digital flat panel detector that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The report is Rev.2 of document A-TCF-5-060 for ADD HAD1417MCW and HAD1717MCW.
The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on 2022.12.08.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Reviewed by Approved by Name Kim Wan Su Lee, Dong jin Hur Namkyu Position R&D team R&D Manager Quality Manager Date 2022.12.08 2022.12.08 2022.12.08

evidence.claim: Rev.2 added the family model HAD1717MCW.
The manufacturer is H&abyz Co.,Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd. Address 1F, 2-dong, 41-16, cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The ADD device is classified as Class IIa under Rule 16 of Annex IX of MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(HAD1417MCW / HAD1717MCW), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The IFU contraindications include mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Target Patient Population A Adult and children except infant and pregnant women

evidence.claim: The device uses a-Si TFT sensor technology and CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: Item HAD1417MCW HAD1717MCW Sensor Type a-Si TFT Scintillator CsI

evidence.claim: The HAD1417MCW total pixel area is 358.5 x 430 mm and HAD1717MCW total pixel area is 430 x 430 mm.
The device supports IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n Wi-Fi, 1Giga-bit Ethernet communication, Normal Mode, and AED Mode.
evidence.evidenceText: Wi-Fi IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n Communication 1Giga-bit Ethernet Trigger Mode Normal Mode AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode)

evidence.claim: The report states compliance with IEC 60601-1 limits for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD has been tested and found to comply with the limits for medical devices in IEC 60601-1.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation considered MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 and MEDDEV 2.12/2 Rev.2.
evidence.evidenceText: MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 Clinical Evaluation MEDDEV 2.12/2 Rev.2 Post Market Clinical Follow-up Studies

evidence.claim: The literature search used PubMed and ScienceDirect, with search dates from 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15.
evidence.evidenceText: The “PUBMED” and “Science Direct” were utilized to search for literature... Date of Search: 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15

evidence.claim: No pre-market clinical investigations or PMCF studies sponsored by the manufacturer were listed as generated clinical data.
evidence.evidenceText: All pre-market clinical investigations N/A - PMCF studies, such as post market clinical investigations and any device registries sponsored by the manufacturer N/A -

evidence.claim: The report used equivalent products including DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, and S4335AW.
evidence.evidenceText: We used as the clinical evaluation data the follow device, -DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, S4335AW

evidence.claim: The report concludes that ADD is not applying new technology but widely used technology.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD is not a device applying new technology, but it is a device applying widely used technology.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the device can be expected to exhibit the claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.
evidence.evidenceText: Considering the literature review, safety & performance test and comparison to similar devices, we concluded that this device can be expected to exhibit the claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the risks associated with device use are acceptable when weighed against patient benefits.
evidence.evidenceText: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous for the specific registration context: sourcePath mentions Peru/DIGEMID, but the document text primarily supports MDD, CE marking, competent authorities, and notified bodies.
Sales approval status and sales volume include multiple TBD entries for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., China, 2019 sales, and vigilance categories.

Potentially conflicting patient population statements: IFU target population excludes infants and pregnant women, while later analysis says ADD can be considered to have no age limitation based on CXDI-31 literature.

Text quality issues are present, including duplicate literature numbering for #004/#005, incomplete sentence fragments, typographical errors, and inconsistent standard references such as IEC 62220-1 versus EN 62220-1-1.
Some equivalence evidence relies on predicate device literature and manufacturer assertions rather than direct clinical investigations of ADD.
